clusters.title: The Schools Giving Children a Say in How They Are Run
clusters.summary: When a six-year-old suggests a "buddy bench" for lonely classmates and watches it become real, something shifts - not just in the schoolyard, but in how that child understands their own place in the world. Smart School Councils, a UK charity founded by former teacher Greg Sanderson, has built a simple platform that gives even the youngest pupils a structured voice in shaping their school life, with nearly 90% of participating students now reporting that they feel listened to. The premise is modest but the stakes are not: as trust in democratic institutions erodes and young people grow increasingly disenchanted with politics, Sanderson believes that civic confidence must be cultivated long before anyone is old enough to vote.

items.title: The Schools Giving Children a Say in How They Are Run
items.description: When a six-year-old suggests a "buddy bench" for lonely classmates and watches it become real, something shifts - not just in the schoolyard, but in how that child understands their own place in the world. Smart School Councils, a UK charity founded by former teacher Greg Sanderson, has built a simple platform that gives even the youngest pupils a structured voice in shaping their school life, with nearly 90% of participating students now reporting that they feel listened to. The premise is modest but the stakes are not: as trust in democratic institutions erodes and young people grow increasingly disenchanted with politics, Sanderson believes that civic confidence must be cultivated long before anyone is old enough to vote. As assistant headteacher Ellie Nott puts it, "If you can look back and say, 'I had an idea when I was six, and somebody listened, and then it happened', that will stand children in good stead, hopefully, to vote when they're older." What the project quietly reveals is that democracy doesn't fail first at the ballot box - it fails when people never learn, in their bones, that their voice can change anything at all.

items.title: The schools giving children a say in how they are run
items.description:
As trust in democracy declines, a project putting pupils’ voices at the heart of school life is proving its worth

The post The schools giving children a say in how they are run appeared first on Positive News.

clusters.title: Mister Rogers Stamps to Be Reissued by USPS After Contest Win
clusters.summary: The United States Postal Service has announced the reissuance of the popular Mister Rogers stamps, following their victory in a recent encore contest. These stamps, celebrating the beloved children's television icon, will be available for purchase starting in June. The decision reflects ongoing public affection for Mister Rogers and his contributions to children's programming.

items.title: Mister Rogers Stamp Wins USPS Contest, Will Now Be Reissued
Beloved Mister Rogers stamps reissued by USPS after winning 'encore contest'
items.description: The beloved “Mister Rogers’ Neighborhood” stamps will be available to purchase in June.

clusters.title: Good News in History, June 7
clusters.summary: 18 years ago today, Iceland enshrined the area surrounding Vatnajökull glacier as a national park, the second-largest in all of Europe behind Yugyd Va in Russia. The Vatnajökull glacier is the largest in Europe outside the Arctic, but the national park includes two areas that were previously protected called Skaftafell and Jökulsárgljúfur. READ about the area’s […] The post Good News in History, June 7 appeared first on Good News Network.

items.title: Good News in History, June 6
items.description:
80 years ago today, the National Basketball Association (NBA) was created with 11 teams. Interestingly, it was founded in New York City by owners of the major ice hockey arenas. First known as the Basketball Association of America, the league changed its name to the National Basketball Association in 1949, after merging with the competing […]

clusters.title: Thousands Donate to Help Nebraska Ranchers Who Couldn’t Feed Their Herds After Wildfires Burn Every Acre
clusters.summary: A few months ago the largest wildfire in Nebraska history burned a thousand square-miles of ranch land. It burned every foot of grass on Mike and Kayla Wintz’s 11,000-acre ranch. But when they and their neighbors faced the threat of losing their livelihoods, something remarkable happened.

clusters.title: Nature’s Beauty is Restored After Farmer Obliterated Shoreline of Important Salmon Run
clusters.summary: Before-and-after photos from Herefordshire, England, show how nature’s beauty has returned to a riverside previously obliterated by a local farmer. The farmer used an 18-ton digger to dredge a section of the River Lugg near Leominster, and stripped every tree from a mile-long stretch of one of Britain’s most important salmon rivers. He was jailed […] The post Nature’s Beauty is Restored After Farmer Obliterated Shoreline of Important Salmon Run appeared first on Good News Network.
